title: I found 200 Crude Gold Bars in a single spot in Crimson Desert, which amounts to 1,500 silver for only ten minutes of trouble
summary: As long as you sell them to the right merchant.
body: Adding Crude Gold Bars to Crimson Desert is one way that Pearl Abyss is trying to make the game's treasure a bit more exciting. Before, you'd often find a hoard of glittering gold bars, coins, and trinkets, but none of it was actually treasure, just part of the set dressing. Sure, you could find proper Gold Bars , but these are pretty rare unless you know where to look, and even crafting them is costly in the extreme. Crude Gold Bars are basically just a stackable form of treasure that you can sell to traders, and it makes exploring and looting enemies more lucrative. You can gather around 200 Crude Gold Bars in the Harpy Nest in Pailune (Image credit: Pearl Abyss) One spot in particular is a veritable hoard of Crude Gold Bars, and that's the Harpy Nest in the northern region of Pailune, a fair way southeast of Pailune City at the top of a tall mountain, just west of the "I" in Pailune on the map. This location has boxes filled with Crude Gold Bars, piles of them, and the odd bar dotted about in the snow—it takes a little while to gather these, but by my count, there are around 200 Crude Gold Bars in the nest, depending on how thorough you are. There are boxes filled with bars but…

title: With TV News In Free Fall, Anchors Try Breaking Away
summary: As cost cuts roil the networks, A-list talent (and their agents) are mulling ways to go indie to make up for slashed salaries — a move that's not only risky but expensive.

title: Pakistan Issues Emergency LNG Tender for Two Cargoes as Power Crisis Deepens
summary: Pakistan on Wednesday issued a tender seeking to urgently buy two LNG cargoes for delivery later this month as the country struggles to cope with a gas and power crisis amid disrupted LNG supplies from the Middle East. Pakistan LNG Limited (PLL), the state-owned company procuring LNG from internati…

title: Coal in Old School RuneScape has, in theory, outperformed cryptocurrency Ethereum this year
body: We're all pretty aware that crypto probably isn't, erm, what you'd call a safe investment for the future. Especially since, as of earlier this year, it's apparently in a "collateral death spiral" . Which doesn't sound great. But if you made a different bet—say, by purchasing Old School RuneScape bonds, converting them into gp, and then ruthlessly violating the terms of service to peddle that gold to players for real cash, you would've outperformed one of the biggest cryptocurrencies, Ethereum, this year. To be clear, I'm not saying you should've done that. But hypothetically if you did, and you were able to find a buyer that'd match the money you put into those bonds to buy coal 1:1, it is a statistical fact you would've made more moolah. That's per X account Arkham , which tracks all sort of cryptocurrency trading stuff (thanks, Eurogamer ). According to the graphs shown below, Coal grew around 40 percent, whereas Ethereum only grew in the low 30s. Ethereum, of course, being one of the main currencies that was part of the crypto coldrush circa 2021 . If you bought Coal on Runescape 1 year ago you outperformed Ethereum pic.twitter.com/oBrRgND1ET May 4, 2026 And coal, of course, be…

title: Oil Futures Markets Still Too Complacent About Supply Shock
summary: The oil futures market appears not to have caught up with the massive supply disruption from the Middle East yet—a supply shock that is now in its third month and lasting more than anyone expected in early March. Brent and WTI crude futures this week are trading more than $30 per barrel higher comp…
